title: Ericoides cyrilliflorum

This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyrtophyllum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cyrtophyllum include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Ericoides cyrilliflorum is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. This family includes other plants such as heathers and rhododendrons. The Ericaceae family is part of the larger order Ericales, which is a group of flowering plants that are commonly found in temperate climates. Ericales is also known as the heath order. The classification of Ericoides cyrilliflorum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in these groups.
